goal: Generate a short dramatic-comedy dialogue story between an astronaut and a robot assistant, based on a user-provided prompt, saved as a Markdown file.
user_persona: A casual or hobbyist writer who wants quick, entertaining short-form story content from a simple topic prompt.

sub_tasks.description: Accept a short user-provided text prompt describing the topic or scenario for the story.
Generate a short dramatic-comedy dialogue between two fixed characters: an astronaut and a robot assistant, grounded in the provided topic.
Ensure the generated dialogue maintains a dramatic-comedy tone — balancing emotional tension with humor throughout the exchange.
Format the final dialogue story as a well-structured Markdown document with a title, character labels, and dialogue lines.
Save the Markdown output as a .md file in the workspace directory, with a filename derived from the prompt or a timestamp.

privacy: No external API calls beyond the LLM provider
No user data stored outside the workspace directory
autonomy_limits: Must not overwrite an existing .md file without warning
Must not alter any files outside the workspace directory
Characters are fixed: one astronaut and one robot assistant — do not change them without explicit user instruction

success_criteria: Given a short prompt, a .md file is produced in the workspace directory within 30 seconds.
The output file contains a clearly formatted dialogue with labeled turns for both the astronaut and the robot assistant.
The tone of the dialogue reads as dramatic-comedy — reviewable by the user without further editing needed.
The filename of the output is human-readable and traceable to the triggering prompt or run timestamp.
assumptions: Output language defaults to Spanish, matching the user's interaction language, unless the prompt is provided in English.
Story length defaults to approximately 8–14 dialogue exchanges (short form) unless the user specifies otherwise.
The two characters are always the same: one astronaut (unnamed by default) and one robot assistant (unnamed by default).
The .md file is saved to the workspace directory root unless a subdirectory is specified by the user.
No interactive review step is included by default — the file is written in one shot.
Cost sensitivity is assumed low since output is local and no paid external services beyond the LLM are required.
